title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A tale of wealth, obsession, and the American Dream set in the roaring 1920s, following the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his pursuit of the enchanting Daisy Buchanan.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A must-read handbook for software engineers that teaches practical techniques for writing readable, maintainable, and efficient code that stands the test of time.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An iconic exploration of the universe's biggest mysteries — from the Big Bang to black holes — written for general readers without any background in physics.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical novel following a young Andalusian shepherd on a magical journey to find a worldly treasure, discovering that true wealth lies in following one's personal legend.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt
description: A timeless guide for developers offering practical advice on software craftsmanship, career building, and writing code that adapts to changing requirements and technologies.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A breathtaking journey through space and time, exploring the origins of the universe, the nature of life, and humanity's place among the billions of galaxies.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful story of racial injustice and childhood innocence set in the American South, seen through the eyes of young Scout Finch as her father defends a Black man.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of JavaScript, challenging common misconceptions and revealing the true power of the language that runs the modern web.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A revolutionary examination of evolution from the gene's perspective, introducing the concept of the selfish gene and the cultural equivalent known as the meme.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society under constant surveillance, following Winston Smith as he dares to question the all-powerful Party and its leader Big Brother.

title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
author: Douglas Crockford
description: A concise guide that uncovers the elegant and reliable subset of JavaScript, helping developers avoid the language's pitfalls and write better, more maintainable web applications.

title: The Gene: An Intimate History
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A sweeping narrative of the history of the gene, from Mendel's peas to CRISPR, exploring how this fundamental unit of heredity shapes identity, disease, and the future of humanity.
